meta.topic: 12 documents the ROC actually needs before your Pvt Ltd application
meta.audience: First-time founders registering a Pvt Ltd

slides.headline: 12 documents the ROC actually wants before your Pvt Ltd application.
slides.subhead: Skip one and your application stalls.
Average delay when DSC and DIN aren't sequenced before the main MCA filing. The form accepts — the RoC officer doesn't.

slides.headline: What most guides list vs. what RoC-Kanpur actually checks

left.label: What guides tell you
left.body: PAN, Aadhaar, address proof, passport photos. File SPICe+ and wait.

right.label: What the officer reviews
right.body: DSC validity, DIN status, name availability on MCA + IP India, MOA object clause vs. restricted activities list, subscriber sheet notarization.

slides.headline: The 12 documents — split by who prepares them

left.label: Founder prepares (7)
left.body:
1. PAN card
2. Aadhaar
3. Passport-size photo
4. Address proof (utility bill <2 months)
5. Specimen signature
6. Email + mobile OTP for DSC
7. Registered office address proof

right.label: CS/agent prepares (5)
right.body:
8. DSC (Class 3)
9. DIN (if first directorship)
10. RUN or SPICe+ Part A (name reservation)
11. MOA + AOA drafts
12. INC-9 declaration

slides.headline: The MCA portal accepts your form. Then the RoC officer manually reviews the MOA — that's where most rejections hide.
Want the full checklist?
slides.body: Comment DOCS and I'll DM you the 12-document checklist we give every founder on day 1. No form, no follow-up call required.

caption:
12 documents the ROC actually wants before your Pvt Ltd application.

Most first-time founders come in with PAN, Aadhaar, and a photo — and think that's it.

It isn't.

Here's what actually gets checked when your SPICe+ form lands with the RoC officer:

→ DSC (Class 3) — valid and not expired
→ DIN — active for each director
→ Name availability — MCA search + IP India trademark check
→ MOA object clause — cross-referenced against the RoC's restricted activities list
→ Subscriber sheet — properly notarized, not just signed

The MCA portal will accept a form that's missing half of this. The officer won't.

That's where 4–6 week delays come from. Not the govt. Your prep.

Comment DOCS and I'll DM you the full 12-document checklist we walk every founder through on day 1.

#PvtLtd #CompanyRegistration #IndiaStartup #Founder #Compliance #Noida #MCA
